_doc: Durable queue of pending GPU work for the hourly fleet check (fleet-progress-aws.yml reports FREE SLOTS). On a free spot-Training slot the hourly check dispatches the highest-priority item with auto_launch=true whose preconditions are met, marks it in_flight, and commits this file. Items with auto_launch=false are EXPENSIVE/multi-leg (autonomy threshold) and are surfaced to the user for sign-off, never auto-launched. Keep this file current as work is launched/finished/approved.
_user_hold: 2026-07-10 9:55 PM ET — USER DIRECTIVE: do NOT start any NEW GPU runs this session. In-flight runs (T4L benchmark, nr4a2rep-r1 pilot) continue. No resuming paused replicates, no lambda-repair, no paralogue ABFE, no Phase-2 re-shakeout — surface to the user instead. CPU/$0 work and read-only monitoring are unaffected. Applies to autonomous hourly-Routine firings too (they fire into this session).

items.desc: NR4A2 λ-repair ABFE replicate 2 (complex leg) — PAUSED ~18% for validate-first (prove the dense-16-window schedule restores soft-core-tail MBAR overlap on r1 BEFORE spending on the error-bar replicates)
items.workflow: gpu-abfe-aws.yml (or the nr4a2rep submitter) — re-dispatch with the SAME checkpoint prefix nr4a3-abfe-nr4a2rep-r2 to resume from checkpoint
preconditions: r1 reaches the repaired tail windows (~9-15)
then resume for the error bar
items.note: Stopped 2026-07-10 ~6:05 PM ET. Checkpoint preserved in S3 (resumes from ~18%, loses <=1 window). ** USER HOLD 2026-07-10 9:55 PM ET: NO NEW GPU RUNS THIS SESSION — do NOT resume even if the r1 validation gate (window 9 overlap check) passes. The gate diagnostic (CPU) may still run; if it validates, SURFACE to the user and wait, do NOT re-dispatch. ** Gated on the r1 overlap check AND the user hold.

items.desc: NR4A2 λ-repair ABFE replicate 3 (complex leg) — PAUSED ~17% for validate-first (same gate as r2)
items.workflow: gpu-abfe-aws.yml (or the nr4a2rep submitter) — re-dispatch with the SAME checkpoint prefix nr4a3-abfe-nr4a2rep-r3 to resume from checkpoint
preconditions: r1 reaches the repaired tail windows (~9-15)
then resume for the error bar
items.note: Stopped 2026-07-10 ~6:05 PM ET. Checkpoint preserved. ** USER HOLD 2026-07-10 9:55 PM ET: NO NEW GPU RUNS THIS SESSION — do NOT resume even if the r1 validation gate passes; surface to the user and wait. ** Gated on the r1 overlap check AND the user hold.

items.desc: Phase 2 single-shard shakeout — metad on the data-derived TICA CV, NR4A3 seed 1, 8 ns
items.note: COMPLETE 2026-07-10 9:21 PM ET (full 8 ns, gate passed). RECROSSING = 0. Diagnostic (metad-analysis on s): the biased CV stayed ENTIRELY in the closed basin (frac_below=1.0; explored s range [-2.96,1.14] out of fit grid [-8.98,17.33]; open is the high-s end it never reached). Root cause: the distances-only data-derived CV correlates corr(s,Rg)=0.816 — it passed the lenient 0.90 gate but is too Rg-like, so it behaved like the Rg-metad that already failed to recross (Phase-1 full-feature TICA was 0.68 = genuinely distinct). Also 8 ns << ~17 ns slow timescale. NO-GO for the fleet as-is.

items.desc: Full Phase 2 opening-FE fleet — 8 runs: NR4A3 seeds 2,3 + NR4A1 r1/2/3 + NR4A2 r1/2/3, ~50 ns each, tica_combine CV, to get the paralogue-differential opening free energy
items.workflow: metad-tica-shakeout-aws.yml (per run; TARGET/SEED vary — a fleet submitter can be added when approved)
preconditions: a re-shakeout with an improved CV shows closed<->open recrossing
items.note: BLOCKED 2026-07-10 9:47 PM ET: the shakeout returned 0 recrossings — the distances-only CV (corr(s,Rg)=0.816) is too Rg-like and stayed in the closed basin. The user's contingent pre-approval was for a shakeout that 'comes back looking how we want it'; it did NOT, so the fleet is NOT launched. Redesign before re-shaking: (1) fit the CV with the RICHER feature set (gate chi1 sin/cos + pocket SASA + lining distances) to reach the Phase-1 corr(s,Rg)~0.68, and tighten the fit gate from 0.90 to ~0.75; (2) run a LONGER shard (~20-30 ns > the ~17 ns slow timescale) before committing the fleet. Also fix the recrossing boundary to be data-driven (median of the explored range) not the grid midpoint. Only re-approach the $100-200 fleet after a re-shakeout recrosses.
